records.Summary: The meetup began with introductions at Central Park, Connaught Place, followed by the National Anthem, which filled everyone with pride and unity. We then headed to Haldiram’s, where everyone enjoyed snacks, fun conversations, and bonding, making the atmosphere lively and friendly.

records.Summary: The meetup began with warm introductions  most of us already knew each other, so we connected easily. After some fun clicks, we dived into the Garba night at Pacific Mall, Tagore Garden , where the music, dance, and energy created the perfect vibe for everyone to enjoy and bond.

Project Udaan was organized as Delhi’s first-ever volunteering meetup under the IIT Madras BS Degree Program in collaboration with Sahara – The Welfare Society of IITM BS, Sundarbans House, and Robin Hood Academy (RHA). The initiative aimed to bring together students for meaningful community engagement and social welfare activities. As part of the initiative, volunteers interacted with orphan children through various fun and interactive activities including Kho-Kho, Eye Spy, group games, conversations, and recreational sessions. The event focused on creating a joyful and inclusive environment for the children while encouraging teamwork, compassion, and community bonding among volunteers and participants.

A  fantastic meetup for BS students in Noida, and it was a blast! A huge thanks to everyone who came and made this day so special. New friendships, endless laughter, and unforgettable memories -the day was everything we dreamed of and more!

records.Summary:
Bonfire Bash was pure warmth in every sense. From Live Dhol and DJ to the cozy Bonfire Zone and that unlimited buffet, it was an evening to remember. 
There was something so healing about that winter night, heart is full ✨

records.Summary: bringing the IIT Madras BS Degree Programme community together—no agenda, just students connecting, sharing stories, and building memories beyond screens.

Good times, familiar faces, and an electric energy that makes campus life unforgettable ✨

Sundarbans House connects the IIT Madras BS Degree Programme community, creating a space for students to bond, share experiences, and make memories that transcend screens.

its first-ever girls-only meetup, and the room slowly filled with girls dressed beautifully, carrying their glow and their stories. What started as strangers walking in turned into laughter echoing over clay art, hands busy decorating candles, and compliments being passed around like secrets.

The Delhi Chapter of Rashtriya Khel Mahotsav 2026 was successfully organized through the collaboration of Sundarbans House, Nallamala House, and Sportify. The event brought together IIT Madras BS students for an energetic and engaging sports meetup focused on teamwork, fitness, and community bonding. Participants actively took part in various sports and recreational activities including cricket, badminton, team-based games, and friendly matches that encouraged interaction and collaboration among students. Along with competitive gameplay, the event also created opportunities for networking, group conversations, photography sessions, and informal social interaction, making the meetup both enjoyable and memorable.

Participants spent time exploring the historical architecture of Purana Qila, visiting heritage structures, capturing photographs, engaging in group conversations, and sharing experiences with fellow students. The meetup also included casual networking, interactive discussions, sightseeing, and recreational activities that encouraged collaboration and community bonding among attendees. The event created a lively and welcoming atmosphere filled with meaningful conversations, shared experiences, and enjoyable moments among students.

The meetup provided a vibrant platform for students to interact, network, and strengthen community connections in an enjoyable and informal environment. Participants engaged in various activities including group discussions, casual networking, photography sessions, food outings, mall exploration, and interactive conversations with fellow students. The meetup encouraged collaboration, friendship-building, and community bonding among students from different houses and academic backgrounds.
